text: States (statement -> tail-recursion) #9 is NOT available for this tree recursion (no single tail call)
evidence: "flatten over a tree makes multiple recursive calls per frame (one per nested array), so it's non-tail recursion. Even in an engine with TCO, tail-call elimination can't save a call that isn't in tail position. This is the crux -- you can't rescue tree recursion with a tail-call trick." -- explicitly the branching/no-tail-position structure, separate from the TCO point.
